metadata_information.title: Leader Value Added : Assessing the Growth Contribution of Individual National Leaders
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Leader Value Added : Assessing the Growth Contribution of Individual National Leaders

document_description.abstract: Previous literature suggests that leaders matter for growth in general. This paper asks which leaders matter and develops a methodology to estimate the growth contribution of individual leaders and calculate its precision. The findings show that few leaders have statistically significant contributions; it is difficult to know who is good for growth and who is not. The paper also finds that the most intuitive estimate of a leader's contribution -- the average growth rate during tenure -- is largely useless for measuring his or her true contribution. Consequently, many leaders with statistically significant growth effects are surprises. Moreover, leaders in non-democratic countries are no more likely to be statistically significant than leaders in democratic ones.
